prompt: IPMVP Option B is characterized by:

options.text: Retrofit isolation with utility-grade measurement of energy use at the ECM level
Whole-facility utility bill comparison only with no submeters
Computer simulation alone without any field measurement
No baseline period required before implementation

explanation: Option B uses periodic or continuous measurements at specific retrofits to quantify savings.

distractorExplanations.b: Whole-building only is closer to Option C.
distractorExplanations.c: Option D emphasizes simulation; Option B requires measurement.
distractorExplanations.d: All IPMVP options require a baseline concept.

prompt: In an Energy Savings Performance Contract (ESPC), the ESCO typically:

options.text: Designs, installs, and often guarantees savings financed through future energy cost reductions
Provides only landscaping services with no performance risk
Eliminates all owner review of measures
Requires no measurement and verification plan

explanation: ESCOs assume implementation and performance risk per contract, repaid via savings stream.

distractorExplanations.c: Owners retain approval and oversight roles.
distractorExplanations.d: M&V is essential to verify savings.

prompt: The commissioning authority (CxA) on a new construction project should:

options.text: Review design, witness testing, and verify systems operate per owner's project requirements
Approve change orders without owner knowledge only
Skip functional performance testing to save time
Replace the mechanical contractor entirely

explanation: Independent or qualified CxA verifies design intent through plans review, submittals, and testing.

distractorExplanations.d: CxA oversees verification; contractor executes work.

prompt: The design intent document in commissioning:

options.text: Translates owner requirements into measurable performance criteria for systems
Lists only contractor phone numbers
Replaces all O&M manuals permanently
Eliminates the need for any test scripts

explanation: Design intent bridges OPR/BOD to verifiable parameters used during functional testing.

distractorExplanations.b: Contact lists are not design intent.
distractorExplanations.d: Test scripts implement verification against intent.

prompt: Functional performance testing (FPT) demonstrates:

options.text: Systems and assemblies operate according to sequences and setpoints under simulated conditions
Only that equipment nameplates are readable
Structural wind tunnel results exclusively
That no training is needed for operators

explanation: FPT validates controls interlocks, safeties, and modes beyond simple startup.

prompt: Retrocommissioning (RCx) is best defined as:

options.text: Systematic process to improve performance of existing buildings whose systems were not commissioned or have drifted
Commissioning applied only before original construction begins
Replacement of all equipment on day one without testing
Elimination of all BAS trending

explanation: RCx uses documentation review, testing, and control corrections in operating buildings.

distractorExplanations.c: RCx focuses on existing systems, not full replacement by default.

prompt: A commissioning plan typically includes:

options.text: Scope, schedule, roles, test procedures, and acceptance criteria
Only the cafeteria menu for construction workers
Exclusive focus on carpet colors
No documentation of issues or resolutions

explanation: The plan organizes activities from design through occupancy and warranty.

distractorExplanations.c: Finishes are not the Cx plan focus.
distractorExplanations.d: Issues logs document deficiencies and closure.

prompt: Baseline adjustments in M&V account for:

options.text: Changes in static factors (e.g., area, shift schedule) that affect energy use independent of ECM performance
Random selection of any year without documentation
Ignoring all occupancy changes forever
Eliminating weather normalization always

explanation: IPMVP requires documenting adjustments when conditions change so savings remain attributable to ECMs.

distractorExplanations.b: Adjustments must be justified and documented.
distractorExplanations.c: Occupancy changes may require routine adjustments.

prompt: Guaranteed savings in an ESPC means:

options.text: The ESCO commits contractually to meet agreed savings targets, often with remedies if shortfalls occur
Savings are estimated with no verification ever
The owner assumes all performance risk exclusively
Utility rates are fixed for 50 years by law automatically

explanation: Guarantees tie payments and remedies to measured or stipulated savings per M&V plan.

distractorExplanations.d: Rates change; contracts address risk allocation.

prompt: Ongoing commissioning (OCx) differs from one-time RCx because OCx:

options.text: Uses continuous monitoring and periodic re-testing to maintain performance over time
Never uses BAS data or meters
Eliminates all operator training
Applies only to buildings less than one year old

explanation: OCx persists beyond a single RCx event using analytics and scheduled checks.

distractorExplanations.d: OCx applies to buildings of any age.

prompt: Testing, adjusting, and balancing (TAB) primarily ensures:

options.text: Hydronic and air systems deliver design flows and pressures
All architectural finishes meet VOC limits
Elevator traffic studies are complete
Roof warranty is registered

explanation: TAB verifies distribution performance; commissioning verifies overall system function and sequences.

prompt: Owner's Project Requirements (OPR) define:

options.text: Owner goals for performance, comfort, energy, and operability used to guide design and commissioning
Contractor profit margins exclusively
Only paint colors for lobby walls
Manufacturer stock ticker symbols

explanation: OPR captures what the owner needs; basis of design shows how the team meets OPR.

distractorExplanations.c: Aesthetics alone do not define OPR.

prompt: A final commissioning report should include:

options.text: Test results, issues resolved, training provided, and recommendations for O&M
Only unsigned blank pages
Deletion of all test data for privacy without replacement
No reference to design intent or OPR

explanation: The report documents verification outcomes and handoff to operations.

prompt: Defining the measurement boundary in M&V means:

options.text: Specifying which meters and systems are included in savings calculations
Drawing the property line on a site plan only for landscaping
Ignoring any interactive effects between ECMs
Eliminating all baseline data collection

explanation: Clear boundaries prevent double counting and gaps in savings attribution.

distractorExplanations.b: Property line alone does not define energy boundaries.

prompt: Regression models for whole-building M&V typically use:

options.text: Utility consumption regressed against weather and other independent variables
Only random number generation without variables
Module color as the sole predictor
Filter MERV as dependent variable for kWh

explanation: Regression baselines normalize consumption to weather for fair pre/post comparison.

distractorExplanations.c: Module color does not predict building kWh.
distractorExplanations.d: Filter rating is not a standard regression term for utility use.

prompt: Stipulated savings in M&V are:

options.text: Savings assigned by agreement without direct measurement of that specific ECM
Always preferred over any measurement per IPMVP in all cases
Illegal in all ESPC contracts nationwide
Identical to Option B isolation metering always

explanation: Stipulated values may be used when measurement is impractical, with justification in the M&V plan.

distractorExplanations.b: Stipulated savings are allowed with documentation.
distractorExplanations.c: They are used in many contracts.
distractorExplanations.d: Option B uses measured isolation, not stipulated by definition.

prompt: Recommissioning is typically performed:

options.text: Periodically after initial commissioning to restore performance as systems change
Only once before design begins and never again
Without any comparison to original design intent
Only on equipment with zero operating hours

explanation: Recommissioning addresses drift, renovations, and operational changes over building life.

prompt: Commissioning agent independence is important to:

options.text: Provide objective verification separate from design and construction teams when required by owner or LEED
Ensure the CxA always profits from change orders
Eliminate all owner involvement in testing
Prevent any documentation of deficiencies

explanation: Independence reduces conflicts of interest in identifying issues affecting energy and operability.

distractorExplanations.b: CxA should not profit from unnecessary changes.

prompt: ESPC financing is often considered off-balance-sheet for public owners when:

options.text: Structured so the obligation resembles a service payment tied to savings, per applicable accounting rules
The owner pays 100% upfront cash day one always
No third party is involved in any case
M&V is explicitly prohibited

explanation: Accounting treatment depends on contract terms and applicable standards; ESPCs aim to avoid capital budget barriers.

prompt: Utility incentives tied to retrofits commonly require:

options.text: Pre- and post-verification, often aligned with IPMVP or program-specific M&V rules
No documentation of baseline conditions
Permanent elimination of all meters
Ignore measure installation dates

explanation: Programs verify savings claims before paying incentives.

prompt: Weather normalization in M&V adjusts consumption so that:

options.text: Comparisons reflect ECM impacts rather than year-to-year weather differences
Hotter summers automatically prove savings without analysis
Heating degree days are ignored in all climates
Baseline year can be changed monthly without notice

explanation: Normalization uses degree-days or regression so performance is evaluated fairly.

distractorExplanations.b: Weather alone does not prove savings.

prompt: IPMVP Option C is most appropriate when:

options.text: Whole-facility or whole-system utility data are used with routine adjustments
Only a single lamp is retrofit with no utility data
No baseline period exists
Simulation replaces all utility bills without calibration

explanation: Option C uses whole-facility metering with adjustments for static factors.

distractorExplanations.b: Small isolated measures may use Option A or B.
distractorExplanations.d: Option D uses calibrated simulation; Option C uses utility data.

prompt: Calibration intervals for revenue-grade or M&V meters should be:

options.text: Defined in the M&V plan per manufacturer and program requirements
Never performed once installed
Only when meters read zero forever
Ignored if utility bills exist

explanation: Accurate metering underpins defensible savings claims.

distractorExplanations.c: Zero readings indicate failure needing service.
distractorExplanations.d: Bills alone may lack granularity for ECM isolation.

prompt: Commissioning handoff to O&M should include:

options.text: Training, as-built sequences, trend access, and documented setpoints
Deletion of all BAS passwords without replacement
No emergency contact list
Removal of all O&M manuals from site

explanation: Operators need documentation and training to sustain commissioned performance.

distractorExplanations.b: Credentials must transfer securely to owners.

prompt: The ESPC performance period typically spans:

options.text: Years after construction/implementation when savings are measured and guaranteed
Only the bid submission week
The design charrette day exclusively
Zero years because M&V is optional always

explanation: Performance periods often run 5–15 years depending on contract and measure life.
